6302ccab3e73f93749c0d009.question: This poet wrote that “I’m to go back to my gate, anyhow” in a poem that opens by stating “To us, separation is just entertainment.” This author claimed that “I was not here for hundreds of years, / But nothing changed for ages here” in a poem in which Tashkent is referred to as “my dear Asian home,” “The Moon in a Zenith.” “The flame has drowned in crystal” in one poem by this author in which the speaker asks “what have poets, in any case, to do with sin” and calls the foulest of sinners “grace incarnate” when compared with (*) “Prince Charming.” This poet questioned how the “white nights” would appear to her son, who was imprisoned for seventeen months, in a poem including the section “Crucifixion.” For 10 points, name this Russian author of “Poem Without a Hero” and “Requiem.”
6302ccab3e73f93749c0d009.question_sanitized: This poet wrote that "I'm to go back to my gate, anyhow" in a poem that opens by stating "To us, separation is just entertainment." This author claimed that "I was not here for hundreds of years, / But nothing changed for ages here" in a poem in which Tashkent is referred to as "my dear Asian home," "The Moon in a Zenith." "The flame has drowned in crystal" in one poem by this author in which the speaker asks "what have poets, in any case, to do with sin" and calls the foulest of sinners "grace incarnate" when compared with (*) "Prince Charming." This poet questioned how the "white nights" would appear to her son, who was imprisoned for seventeen months, in a poem including the section "Crucifixion." For 10 points, name this Russian author of "Poem Without a Hero" and "Requiem."

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d012.question: Gyula Benczur showed this figure bare chested with his left arm obscured and his head leaning downwards and to the left as his right hand grasps towards the viewer. Caravaggio showed him with both of his hands planted on the ground as he leans forwards. John Waterhouse depicted him in a painting that also shows a woman to the left with one bare breast in a pink dress looking back towards him; in that painting, he wears a crown of plants and bends over a (*) pool. The best known rendering of this figure shows a checkerboard in the upper right and features yellow and white outlines of a kneeling person. For 10 points, name this figure whose metamorphosis was shown by Salvador Dali, a person from Greek myth who became enamoured of his own reflection.

One man in this painting is trying to refill his pipe from a brightly colored blue tobacco pouch, while a figure in the back of the foreground appears to be leaning limply forwards. This work brought its artist the repute that allowed him to join the group called The Wanderers. A (*) fair-haired boy in the the middle of this painting dressed in red is seen looking off intently into the distance in contrast to his comrades, who are more despondent. The main subject of this painting is a group of eleven laborers, while a ship with an unfurled sail appears further back. For 10 points, name this Ilya Repin painting showing some men dragging that ship up the namesake river.

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d085.question: This book repeatedly glorifies the present rather than the past, as evidenced by a scene in which the main character changes a phrase in a poem about a past ruler to refer to the current one. One scene in this book sees the main characters bet on how long it will take a mound of snow to melt. A key concept in this work, often translated as (*) “amusing,” is okashi, which is used in its opening passage to describe the flight of two fireflies and the sprinkling of rain. Set during the reign of the Empress Teishi, its author may have written the Matsushima Diary after leaving the imperial court. This work is known for its essays, diary passages, and lists, or Dan, which are catalogues of objects based on a common noun or adjective. For 10 points, name this work of the Heian period written by Sei Shonagon.
6302ccab3e73f93749c0d085.question_sanitized: This book repeatedly glorifies the present rather than the past, as evidenced by a scene in which the main character changes a phrase in a poem about a past ruler to refer to the current one. A key concept in this work, often translated as (*) "amusing," is okashi, which is used in its opening passage to describe the flight of two fireflies and the sprinkling of rain. Set during the reign of the Empress Teishi, its author may have written the Matsushima Diary after leaving the imperial court. This work is known for its essays, diary passages, and lists, or Dan, which are catalogues of objects based on a common noun or adjective. For 10 points, name this work of the Heian period written by Sei Shonagon.
6302ccab3e73f93749c0d085.answer_sanitized: The Pillow Book [or Makura no Soshi]

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d087.question: Eduard Fraenkel analyzed the works of this playwright by noting his use of comparative idioms, claiming that many speeches that he wrote compare mortals favorably to deities with no mythological basis. In one of this man’s plays, the slave Palaestrio invents the character of Honoria as part of a plan to trick Pyrgopolynices into returning Palaestrio to his former master. The title character of one of this man’s plays hires someone to impersonate the messenger, Harpax, as part of his plot to rescue (*) Phoenicium for his master, Calidorus. The creator of an egotistical soldier in Miles Gloriosus, he wrote about the bestowing of a mantle to the prostitute Erotium as part of the confusion in a play about twins that inspired A Comedy of Errors. For 10 points, name this Roman author of Pseudolus and Menaechmi.

For 10 points, name this Roman author of Pseudolus and Menaechmi.

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d093.question: In one painting by this artist, a black robed man to the left has his arms crossed and is about to be whipped, while a figure in blue and orange to the right is being run through with a sword. He paired the four gospel writers with the four Doctors of the Church in the corners of a fresco for a dome that shows Christ appearing to fall from the center. This artist of Martyrdom of Four Saints and (*) Vision of St. John on Patmos depicted a spiralling series of figures that appear to jump out towards the viewer in his Assumption of the Virgin. He also showed a topless woman in a work in which an angel looks up towards a shower of gold and he painted a nude woman from behind being ravished by a nebulous cloud. For 10 points, name this painter of a series of works about the loves of Jupiter.

For 10 points, name this painter of a series of works about the loves of Jupiter.

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d0b0.question: In one painting by this artist whose name references a fortress in Istanbul, a woman leans through a window to the left with an orange blossom, while the main subject looks downcast and is wearing a red dress with a large white headpiece. Another of his paintings shows a yellow bird in a cage at the upper left while the main subject, a red-haired woman, plays with the strings of a violin attached to the rightmost wall. In addition to (*) The Tune of the Seven Towers and Veronica Veronese, he repainted Alexa Wilding’s face onto the canvas of a work in which she holds a mirror and combs her hair, Lady Lilith. He also depicted a sundial and a red bird holding a large yellow flower in his painting Beata Beatrix. For 10 points, name this Pre-Raphaelite artist, the brother of poet Christina.

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d0b6.question: One of these works by this composer twice contains the dizzying syncopation of playing six notes in the right hand while playing five in the left and uses a portion of the melody from Carl Foeppl’s Ever of Thee. The first of these works contains a bass line written in three-fourths time despite the 4/4 time signature in its setting of the ragtime melody “In the Inn” and was premiered by William Masselos. One of these works is known as the (*) “Three Page” one. The best known of these works repeatedly quotes the opening of Beethoven’s Fifth Symphony, has the player strike the keyboard with a fourteen and three quarter inch long piece of wood, and contains sections titled “Emerson,” “Hawthorne,” and “Thoreau.” For 10 points, name these piano pieces including the second, nicknamed Concord.

One of these works is known as the (*) "Three Page" one. For 10 points, name these piano pieces including the second, nicknamed Concord.
6302ccab3e73f93749c0d0b6.answer: Piano Sonatas by Charles Ives [piano not needed after mentioned]

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d0bd.question: In one poem by this non-Frost author, he compared his feelings to fire and those of his lover to ice, though they do not temper each other’s sentiments. This author wrote that “whenas Death shall all the world subdue, / Our love shall live, and later life renew” in a poem in which the speaker’s fruitless attempts to write his lover’s name in sand are compared to his immortal descriptions of her in verse. He wrote a poem “made in lieu of many ornaments, / With which my love should duly have been decked” and a poem for the (*) Somerset sisters that repeats “Sweet Thames, run softly, till I end my song.” He wrote a cycle of poems about his courtship with Elizabeth Boyle using his namesake type of sonnet. For 10 points, name this author of “Prothalamion” and Amoretti who also wrote The Faerie Queene.

This Renaissance artist based his On the Art of Building on Vitruvius's De Architectura, and this author of treatises on painting and sculpting also designed the facade of the Santa Maria Novella.

parts: Name this malicious character who gets a traveling preacher accused of sexual perversity by his wanton accusations and helps a group of people fend off some Apache Indians by making gunpowder.
Judge Holden appears in this novel that tells of the Kid’s dark adventures through the American west and Mexico.
Blood Meridian was written by this author whose novels No Country for Old Men and The Road have both been recently adapted into films.

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d17f.leadin_sanitized: This work's central panel shows the main subject with crossed arms in a blue dress and a white headdress looking up towards the heavens. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this work of art, originally made at the behest of Frederick III of Saxony. Its other portions show such scenes as the Flight to Egypt, Christ Among the Doctors, and the Via Crucis.
This work, by the same artist as the Seven Sorrows Polyptych, shows a skull and a plaque reading “1513” at the bottom right while a dog runs beneath an armored soldier riding a horse.
This early Renaissance artist executed the Seven Sorrows Polyptych and Knight, Death, and the Devil. He also is known for Melancolia I and his depiction of St. Jerome in his Study.

6302ccab3e73f93749c0d1aa.leadin: For 10 points each, name some photographers who displayed social injustices of their day:
6302ccab3e73f93749c0d1aa.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, name some photographers who displayed social injustices of their day:
parts: This photographer was invited by Ralph Merritt to take such photographs as Tom Kobayashi, Landscape, South Fields and Pleasure Park to showcase conditions in Manzanar, which he turned into the book Born Free and Equal.
She highlighted the effects of the Great Depression on the poor in such photographs as Waiting for Relief Checks, taken in Calipatria, and Migrant Mother.
This man is best known for his Pulitzer-winning photograph of Mary Ann Vecchio screaming out at the shooting of Jeffrey Miller, taken while he was a student at Kent State.
